title: Monsoon Sour Jamaican Pecan Stew
description: A shaggy, sour Jamaican stew inspired by the monsoon season, featuring a rich flavor profile with pecan nuts and a touch of fermentation. This low-sodium stew serves a comforting and satisfying meal.
instructions: Add the ginger, garlic, allspice, and thyme to a large cast iron pot over medium heat.
Sear the pecan nuts for 2-3 minutes, or until fragrant.
Add the scotch bonnet peppers and saut for another minute.
Pour in the coconut milk, vegetable broth, apple cider vinegar, and soy sauce, stirring constantly.
Bring the stew to a simmer and cook for 20-25 minutes, or until the ingredients are tender and the flavors have melded.
Stir in the chopped pecan nuts and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs or toasted pecan nuts, if desired.
